overview.summary: The SolarWinds Compromise campaign targeted the energy sector with a multi-stage attack involving reconnaissance, resource development, initial access via phishing, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and ultimately impact through system disruption.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Stealing sensitive data and disrupting operations

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: Gathering information about the target organization's structure, employees, and publicly exposed systems using OSINT techniques.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: Developing a simple yet effective backdoor or payload tailored for the target environment.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: Using spear-phishing emails to deliver the payload to the target organization's employees.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Executing the payload upon successful delivery via the phishing email.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: Creating a registry key to ensure the payload persists across reboots.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Using known vulnerabilities to gain higher-level permissions on the infected system.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Disabling or removing antivirus software to prevent detection.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: Stealing credentials from memory or other locations to facilitate lateral movement.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: Enumerating system information to understand the target environment.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: Using remote management tools to move between systems within the network.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: Collecting sensitive data such as customer information or operational data.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Transferring collected data out of the network using a simple protocol like HTTP.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Shutting down critical systems or processes to disrupt energy production or distribution.

Scenario.summary: The SolarWinds Compromise campaign demonstrated a highly coordinated and multi-staged attack on the energy sector, utilizing various tactics from reconnaissance to impact to achieve its objectives of data theft and operational disruption.
